text_blocks.text: When Yesun Temur died in Shangdu in 1328,
Tugh Temur was recalled to Khanbaliq by the Qipchaq
commander El Temur. He was installed as the emperor
(Emperor Wenzong) in Khanbaliq, while Yesun Temur's son
Ragibagh succeeded to the throne in Shangdu with the
support of Yesun Temur's favorite retainer Dawlat Shah.
Gaining support from princes and officers in Northern China
and some other parts of the dynasty. Khunbaliq-based Tugh
Temur eventually won the civil war against Ragibagh known
as the War of the Two Capitals. Afterwards, Tugh Temur
abdicated in favour of his brother Kusala, who was backed
by Chagatai Khan Eljigidey, and announced Khanbaliq's
intent to welcome him. However, Kusala suddenly died only
four days after a banquet with Tugh Temur. He was
supposedly killed with poison by El Temur, and Tugh
Temur then remounted the throne. Tugh Temur also
managed to send delegates to the western Mongol Khanates
such as Golden Horde and Ilkhanate to be accepted as
the suzerain of Mongol world. However, he was mainly a
puppet of the powerful official & Temur during his latter
three-year reign. El Temur purged pro-Kusala officials and
brought power to warlords, whose despotic rule clearly
marked the decline of the dynasty.
